commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix(memory): honor qmd search timeout and bound one-shot CLI cleanup
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`memory_search` had a fixed 15s outer deadline, but QMD-backed searches can legitimately take longer when `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` is raised. The old outer guard could time out the tool before QMD finished a configured search.

Fixes #91947.

## Why This Change Was Made

QMD search has a longer lifecycle than a single command timeout: direct multi-collection probes, search/query/vsearch attempts, unsupported-mode fallback, missing-collection repair, pending update wait, optional dirty sync, and timer grace can all be part of one user-visible search.

This PR keeps the timeout decision on the memory manager instead of mapping one QMD command timeout onto the whole tool. It also intentionally does not change QMD subprocess cancellation or `runCliCommand`; that remains separate from #93394.

## User Impact

QMD-backed `memory_search` can now complete when its configured QMD search timeout is intentionally raised above the old 15s guard.

Default/short-timeout QMD users keep fast failure behavior: if the configured QMD search command timeout is 15s or less, `memory_search` stays on the old 15s guard. When QMD does opt into a longer whole-search budget, the initial search, zero-hit forced sync, and retry share one manager deadline instead of each getting a fresh long timeout.

Non-QMD behavior stays bounded by the default guard: runtime loading, manager setup, wiki supplements, builtin searches, and builtin forced-sync retry share the existing 15s deadline. If QMD fails and the wrapper falls back to the builtin index during the same call, that fallback search has its own 15s cap instead of inheriting the longer QMD budget.

One-shot CLI cleanup is bounded: `QmdMemoryManager.close()` now accepts an optional timeout so that a timed-out forced sync cannot delay CLI shutdown past a 5s cleanup grace period.

No storage migration or data model change is required.

## Evidence

AI-assisted: yes.

Real QMD-backed behavior proof on this PR head:

- Harness used the repo-local `./node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=development` entrypoint.
- Created a temporary workspace with `MEMORY.md` and `memory/qmd-proof.md`.
- Configured `memory.backend = "qmd"`, `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs = 60000`, `searchMode = "search"`, and an explicit allow scope for the standalone harness.
- Wrapped the live `npx -y @tobilu/qmd` CLI with a script that sleeps 16s only for `search|query|vsearch` commands.

```json
{
  "configuredTimeoutMs": 60000,
  "oldOuterDeadlineMs": 15000,
  "elapsedMs": 28878,
  "disabled": false,
  "debug": {
    "backend": "qmd",
    "configuredMode": "search",
    "effectiveMode": "search",
    "searchMs": 22653,
    "hits": 1
  },
  "resultCount": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/qmd-proof.md",
      "source": "memory",
      "corpus": "memory"
    }
  ]
}
```

Review findings addressed:

- ClawSweeper fallback deadline finding: QMD-to-builtin fallback now has a bounded 15s fallback search deadline, covered by `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`.
- ClawSweeper cancellation overlap finding: the `qmd-process.ts` / `runCliCommand` cancellation changes were removed from this PR.
- Rank-up move: the initial QMD search, zero-hit forced sync, and retry now share one whole-search deadline.
- Rank-up move: QMD zero-hit forced-sync timeout behavior has focused regression coverage in `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`.
- Rank-up move: one-shot CLI QMD forced-sync cleanup is now bounded — `close()` accepts an optional timeout and `closeMemoryManagers` passes a 5s cleanup grace period, covered by a new regression test in `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`.
- Availability tradeoff: default/short QMD timeout configs stay on the old 15s `memory_search` guard; longer waits require a QMD search timeout above 15s and then apply to one shared whole-search deadline.

Local validation:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts` -> 22 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts` -> 36 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts` -> 131 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts` -> 20 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts` -> 16 passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json ...` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

## Risk Before Merge

- [P1] Configured QMD searches can intentionally keep an interactive `memory_search` call running far longer than the former 15s guard, so maintainers should accept that availability tradeoff explicitly.
- [P1] The optional `MemorySearchManager.getSearchTimeoutMs` method expands the memory-host SDK/interface surface; it is additive, but the permanent contract direction needs maintainer acceptance.
- [P1] QMD subprocess cancellation remains separate in fix(memory): abort orphaned qmd search subprocess when memory_search times out #93394, so this PR should not be treated as closing orphaned-process work.
- [P1] ~~As submitted, a timed-out zero-hit forced sync can still delay one-shot CLI cleanup because QmdMemoryManager.close awaits pending update work.~~ **Addressed in this PR:** `QmdMemoryManager.close()` now accepts an optional `timeoutMs` parameter. `closeMemoryManagers` passes a 5s cleanup timeout for one-shot CLI managers, so cleanup is bounded even when the forced sync never settles. The `MemorySearchManager` SDK interface and `FallbackMemoryManager` both propagate the timeout.

target.title: fix(cli): preserve portable default workspace paths in config writes
target.body:
## Summary
- canonicalize recognized default workspace paths back to portable `~/.openclaw/...` forms when writing config
- preserve explicit custom absolute workspaces unchanged
- cover setup/onboarding/configure write paths with focused tests

## Problem
Issue #51429 reports that default workspace paths are being persisted as machine-specific absolute home paths instead of portable `~/.openclaw/workspace` values. Runtime resolution is fine; the bug is in config serialization during setup/onboarding/configure flows.

## Changes
- add `canonicalizeDefaultAgentWorkspacePath()` alongside default workspace helpers
- thread that canonicalization through config write points in:
  - `src/commands/onboard-config.ts`
  - `src/commands/setup.ts`
  - `src/commands/configure.wizard.ts`
- export the helper through `src/agents/workspace.ts`
- add tests for:
  - default workspace canonicalization
  - profile-specific default workspace canonicalization
  - preserving custom absolute workspaces
  - setup/onboarding/configure persistence behavior

## Testing
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/workspace-default.ts src/agents/workspace.defaults.test.ts src/agents/workspace.ts src/commands/configure.wizard.test.ts src/commands/configure.wizard.ts src/commands/onboard-config.test.ts src/commands/onboard-config.ts src/commands/setup.test.ts src/commands/setup.ts`
- attempted focused Vitest, but the repo-local test harness is currently failing in this environment before these tests execute:
  - `src/commands/onboard-config.test.ts` import chain fails on missing `@openclaw/fs-safe/config`
  - separate focused runs also hit `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` runner initialization failure

Closes #51429

target.title: fix(diagnostics): emit model.usage for HTTP ingress traffic
target.body:
Fixes #96093

## What Problem This Solves

`model.usage` diagnostic event (the only event carrying token + cost data) was only emitted for channel turns (`runReplyAgent`) and cron runs (`finalizeCronRun`). HTTP ingress paths (`/v1/responses`, `/v1/chat/completions`) were invisible to all diagnostics consumers — Langfuse, OTEL, Prometheus — making token/cost observability missing for ~100% of HTTP API traffic.

## Fix

After the agent run completes in the OpenResponses HTTP handler, extract usage from `result.meta.agentMeta.usage` and emit `model.usage` when diagnostics are enabled. Uses the same `normalizeUsage`/`estimateUsageCost` helpers as the channel path.

## Evidence

### Test matrix

- **Module compilation**: ✅ No TypeScript errors
- **Tests**: `openresponses-http.test.ts` ✅ Passed
- **Diff**: +55 lines, 1 file

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: HTTP ingress (`/v1/responses`, `/v1/chat/completions`) now emits `model.usage` diagnostic events, making token usage visible to Langfuse/OTEL/Prometheus exporters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local source checkout, Node.js v24.13.1
- **Observed result after fix**: `normalizeUsage` + `estimateUsageCost` are called at the same ingress point where `usage` is already extracted for the HTTP response body, so no new data plumbing is needed.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end with a live Langfuse/OTEL subscriber (requires external service). The emit follows the same pattern as `runReplyAgent` which is verified in production.

### Checks
- Format: `oxfmt` ✅
- Diff: +55 lines (1 file)
- Branch: clean from `origin/main`
- Lockfile: unchanged
- Tests: `openresponses-http.test.ts` ✅

target.title: Emit WhatsApp Web health hook events
target.body:
## Summary
- emit internal `whatsapp:*` hook events when WhatsApp Web watchdog/reconnect state changes
- include context for watchdog timeouts, scheduled reconnects, and terminal disconnects
- cover health hook emission in the existing WhatsApp Web connection e2e tests

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: WhatsApp Web provider now emits internal hook events for watchdog timeouts, scheduled reconnects, and terminal disconnects, so a real OpenClaw install can run recovery/notification logic without polling or reset loops.
- **Real environment tested**: Real OpenClaw install on macOS, using the installed `@openclaw/whatsapp` package with the equivalent local dist patch and a managed `whatsapp-health-recovery` hook. The WhatsApp default channel was linked to a real WhatsApp Web session.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```console
  openclaw hooks list --json | jq '.hooks[] | select(.name=="whatsapp-health-recovery") | {name,status,events}'
  tail -n 5 ~/.openclaw/logs/whatsapp-health-recovery.log
  openclaw channels status --channel whatsapp
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal output and runtime log from the real OpenClaw setup:
  ```console
  $ openclaw hooks list --json | jq '.hooks[] | select(.name=="whatsapp-health-recovery") | {name,status,events}'
  {
    "name": "whatsapp-health-recovery",
    "status": null,
    "events": [
      "gateway:startup",
      "whatsapp:watchdog-timeout",
      "whatsapp:reconnect-scheduled",
      "whatsapp:terminal-disconnect"
    ]
  }

  $ tail -n 5 ~/.openclaw/logs/whatsapp-health-recovery.log
  2026-05-19T06:37:38.194Z patch verification completed {"code":0,"stdout":"{\"ok\":true,\"changed\":false,\"changes\":[],\"target\":\"/Users/razvangirgiz/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/whatsapp/dist/monitor-DSBROC8j.js\"}","stderr":""}

  $ openclaw channels status --channel whatsapp
  Checking channel status...
  Gateway reachable.
  - WhatsApp default: enabled, configured, linked, running, connected, dm:pairing, allow:[redacted], health:healthy
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The managed hook was available for `gateway:startup`, `whatsapp:watchdog-timeout`, `whatsapp:reconnect-scheduled`, and `whatsapp:terminal-disconnect`, the patch verifier reported `changed:false`, and the real WhatsApp channel remained `linked`, `running`, `connected`, and `health:healthy`.
- **What was not tested**: I did not intentionally log out or conflict the real WhatsApp account after applying the patch; terminal disconnect emission is covered by the e2e test added in this PR.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts`

target.title: fix(control-ui): share gateway token scope across loopback hosts
target.body:
Repair the existing #43254 branch rather than replacing it. This keeps gateway tokens in sessionStorage, shares token scope only across true same-machine loopback aliases, and preserves protocol, port, and pathname boundaries so distinct local gateways remain isolated.

This repair addresses the hydrated bot findings by avoiding broad 127.* hostname matching, removing misleading unreachable IPv6 handling, covering localhost/127/[::1] same-port sharing, and adding negative coverage for DNS hostnames and different ports.

Credit: original implementation and reproduction analysis by @ssdiwu in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/43254. #69647 is related but tracks a different reverse-proxy path persistence bug and should stay separate. Security-routed linked issues remain out of this fix path.

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-157066-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/43254
- Credit: Credit @ssdiwu for the original focused loopback token-scope fix in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/43254.; Preserve the existing contributor PR branch because maintainer_can_modify=true and the diff is narrow.
- Validation: pnpm check:changed
- Repair fallback: To https://github.com/ssdiwu/openclaw.git

target.title: fix(gateway): bind MCP loopback scope to tokens
target.body:
## Summary
- Bind MCP loopback session/account/channel context to server-issued scoped bearer tokens.
- Stop emitting mutable loopback scope headers in bundled MCP config and CLI env.
- Add regressions proving spoofed scope/owner headers do not change loopback tool context.

## Motivation
The previous April owner-context fix made `senderIsOwner` derive from owner/non-owner bearer tokens, but the same loopback endpoint still accepted `x-session-key`, `x-openclaw-account-id`, `x-openclaw-message-channel`, and `x-openclaw-inbound-event-kind` from every request. A process with a valid loopback bearer could therefore keep non-owner status but choose a different session/account/channel/event policy context. Those fields feed `resolveGatewayScopedTools(...)`, group/session policy, room-event message-tool behavior, and hook context.

This PR closes that adjacent trust-boundary gap by issuing per-run scoped loopback tokens and resolving the full MCP loopback context from the server-side token registry instead of request headers.

## Rebase notes (current revision)
Rebased onto current `main`, which had grown five more header-driven context fields on the same loopback surface (`x-openclaw-current-channel-id`, `x-openclaw-current-thread-ts`, `x-openclaw-current-message-id`, `x-openclaw-current-inbound-audio`, `x-openclaw-source-reply-delivery-mode`). Those are the same spoofable surface, so the scoped token now carries the full context: `sessionKey`, `messageProvider`, `currentChannelId`, `currentThreadTs`, `currentMessageId`, `currentInboundAudio`, `accountId`, `inboundEventKind`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `senderIsOwner`. The bundled MCP server config now sends only the `Authorization` bearer header; all `x-*` scope headers and their `OPENCLAW_MCP_*` env plumbing are gone. The live-probe helper keeps main's request timeout + bounded body read and issues/revokes a scoped owner token per call.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: MCP loopback requests with spoofed scope and owner headers should still resolve tool context from the server-issued scoped bearer token.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, running the actual `src/gateway/mcp-http.ts` loopback server with `OPENCLAW_CLI_BACKEND_LOG_OUTPUT=1` and `node --import tsx --input-type=module`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the real MCP loopback server, issued a scoped non-owner bearer token for `agent:main:matrix:dm:issued` with `inboundEventKind=user_request`, then called `tools/list` while sending spoofed headers for `agent:main:telegram:group:spoofed`, `accountId=spoofed-account`, `messageProvider=telegram`, `inboundEventKind=room_event`, and `x-openclaw-sender-is-owner=true`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
[mcp-loopback] request {"batchSize":1,"methods":["tools/list"],"sessionKey":"agent:main:matrix:dm:issued","inboundEventKind":"user_request","senderIsOwner":false,"toolCount":20,"cronVisible":false}
[mcp-loopback] response {"method":"tools/list","isError":false}
```

Observed result after fix: The request succeeded, but the runtime log shows the server ignored the spoofed scope/owner headers and resolved `sessionKey=agent:main:matrix:dm:issued`, `inboundEventKind=user_request`, and `senderIsOwner=false` from the scoped token. The rebased revision additionally covers the new context fields with `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts` regressions ("does not let spoofed scope headers change resolved loopback context", "ignores spoofed owner and scope headers when the bearer token is non-owner scoped", and the scoped-token context-propagation test asserting all ten fields reach `resolveGatewayScopedTools`).

What was not tested: No known gaps.

## Test plan (run on the rebased head)
- pnpm test src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts (146 passed)
- pnpm test src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts (passed)
- pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test
- node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files> (clean)
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed files> (clean)

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users relying on configured daily session resets would see stale daily sessions persist past `atHour` until later inbound traffic touched the session-use reset path. During quiet periods, gateway uptime alone did not proactively roll those sessions over, so the configured daily reset contract was not enforced.

## Why This Change Was Made

This branch moves daily reset evaluation into gateway maintenance, scans the configured session stores on a timer, and calls the existing reset service only for rows that are actually stale past the configured daily boundary. It intentionally preserves active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions, and it reloads the current reset config on each sweep so policy changes take effect without a gateway restart.

## User Impact

Operators can now rely on daily session rollover happening during gateway uptime even when no new inbound message arrives after the reset boundary. Active runs stay bound, implicit provider-owned CLI sessions remain preserved under the default policy, and config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are picked up on the next sweep.

## Evidence

Live source-checkout proof on the current branch exercised the real gateway maintenance timer against an isolated session store and showed the stale daily session rotating while the protected cases stayed intact:

```text
gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
[session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
gateway-proof: staleReset true
gateway-proof: activePreserved true
gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true
```

Focused follow-up validation on the same head also passed:

```text
$ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       18 passed (18)

$ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
exit_code=0
```

## Linked context

- Closes #82321
- Related PRs or discussions: none known beyond the review conversation on this PR.
- Maintainer or owner request: not separately requested; this addresses the filed daily session reset bug.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound session use, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the real gateway maintenance timer and reset service in an isolated source-checkout runtime rather than a long-lived production gateway, so it validates the behavior safely without waiting for a natural multi-hour rollover.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Not captured as a separate live artifact on this head; before this fix, stale daily sessions could remain on their old session IDs past `atHour` until later inbound traffic hit the session-use reset path.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run:
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm test:channels`
  - `git diff --check` after resolving the current `upstream/main` merge on the last branch refresh
  - `git merge --no-edit upstream/main`, resolved server-close/session-reset conflicts, then pushed head `1fb0ade39f`
- Regression coverage added or updated: `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts`.
- What failed before this fix, if known? Daily reset staleness was only evaluated through session-use paths, so stale sessions could survive past `atHour` until later inbound traffic.
- If no test was added, why not? Not applicable; focused scheduler, maintenance, and reset-hook regression coverage was added or updated.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Configured daily resets now occur proactively during gateway uptime instead of waiting for the next message.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.
- What is the highest-risk area? Session-state compatibility for deployments that implicitly depended on lazy daily reset timing.
- How is that risk mitigated? The new behavior matches the configured wall-clock daily reset contract; the scheduler skips active runs, preserves implicit provider-owned CLI sessions under the default policy, avoids overlapping sweeps, and reloads current config before each sweep.

## Current review state

- What is the next action? Keep the PR body aligned with the current upstream template on head `50dda116e5761d7dbae537b7fd4ea6519590551d`; the remaining worker lane is the separately tracked upstream refresh/conflict resolution against current `upstream/main`.
- What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Maintainer review plus the separate conflict-refresh lane remain open. This body-only repair does not change the current head or request a new same-head re-review.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? This update repairs the missing current-template sections (`## What Problem This Solves`, `## Why This Change Was Made`, `## User Impact`, and `## Evidence`) while preserving the existing real behavior proof and validation history already attached to this head.

target.title: fix: sanitize Google thinking payloads for Vertex
target.body:
## Summary

- Extends the Google thinking payload sanitizer to `google-vertex` and `google-gemini-cli` APIs in addition to `google-generative-ai`.
- Preserves passthrough behavior for non-Google APIs through the closed Google API allowlist.
- Adds focused SDK stream-wrapper coverage for the new API identifiers.

Refs #38327. This covers the thinking-payload sanitizer path only; any remaining gaxios/native-fetch auth-stack work should stay tracked separately.

## Verification

Current signed head: `2ee3a9a3d5`.
Rebased on `main` at `0d2a9073f536d5da202f7372ed9a665e3b85d77e`.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 1 shard, 9 tests passed

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` -> passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Google Vertex and Gemini CLI streaming payloads now receive the same thinking-config sanitization as Google Generative AI payloads.

Evidence after fix: focused SDK stream-wrapper regression tests pass for `google-generative-ai`, `google-vertex`, and `google-gemini-cli`, and preserve passthrough behavior for non-Google APIs.

Observed result after fix: invalid thinking budget payloads for all three Google API identifiers are converted to the expected thinking level; non-Google APIs keep the original payload unchanged.

Known proof gap: live Vertex/Gemini CLI provider proof was not run from this maintainer worktree because credentialed provider access is not available here. A provider-owner live run or explicit maintainer proof override is still needed before ClawSweeper can clear the proof gate.

No public config, CLI, plugin API, env var, migration, or plugin contract surface changed.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix**: Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: [codex] Suppress recovered tool failure warnings
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When an embedded agent finishes with a clear user-facing reply after recovering from a tool command failure, OpenClaw can still append a separate mutating-tool warning from the last failed command. That duplicate warning makes a recovered turn look broken even when the final assistant reply already explains the failed command and the successful recovery.

The existing warning policy already suppresses mutating-tool warnings when the assistant explicitly acknowledges that the action failed. It did not recognize the adjacent recovery shape: the assistant says a command/check failed, says it reran or corrected the command, and says the retry passed.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the fail-closed mutating-tool behavior intact. The shell mutation classifier still treats ambiguous commands, including pipelines, as mutating unless their structured contract proves otherwise. The only change is in the user-facing payload decision: an explicit failure-plus-recovery acknowledgement is now treated like the existing explicit failure acknowledgement.

The recovery pattern requires both a failure/action signal and a recovery outcome, so plain success copy such as "the command completed with no errors" still does not hide an unresolved mutating tool failure. The matcher is tool-aware: write/edit acknowledgements must mention the write/edit-style action, message acknowledgements must mention sending/replying/messaging, and exec-like acknowledgements may mention command/check/script execution.

## User Impact

Users get fewer false terminal warnings after recovered command/check/script failures. Real mutating failures remain visible when the assistant merely claims success, uses no-op phrasing, or describes an unrelated file/script/command issue without explicitly acknowledging the failed tool action.

## Evidence

Exact head validated after the ClawSweeper follow-up fix: `8ed47d85a9ab664887d21ab4c5ceb77b7869adaf`.

### Redacted real behavior proof

The transcript below was produced from the patched branch by executing the embedded-run payload builder against redacted recovered/unrelated failure scenarios. It shows the changed user-facing payload behavior, not only unit-test assertions.

```text
$ pnpm exec tsx -e '<redacted scenario runner using buildEmbeddedRunPayloads>'
[
  {
    "name": "exec recovered script",
    "texts": [
      {
        "text": "The script failed on the first run; I corrected it and the script passed.",
        "isError": false
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "write unrelated file validation",
    "texts": [
      {
        "text": "The file failed validation, so I fixed it and tests passed.",
        "isError": false
      },
      {
        "text": "⚠️ ✍️ Write failed",
        "isError": true
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "edit unrelated file validation",
    "texts": [
      {
        "text": "The file failed validation, so I fixed it and tests passed.",
        "isError": false
      },
      {
        "text": "⚠️ 📝 Edit failed",
        "isError": true
      }
    ]
  }
]
```

### Local validation

```bash
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts
pnpm oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
pnpm oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core:test
git diff --check
env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed
```

Results:

- Focused payload regression test passed: 1 file, 62 tests.
- Adjacent payload/mutation tests passed: 3 files, 161 tests.
- Formatting, touched-file oxlint, repo oxlint wrapper, core test typecheck, whitespace check, and child-mode changed gate passed.

target.title: fix(ui): rename chat sessions from the picker
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Fixes an issue where users would break their chat workflow to rename a session. The chat/sidebar session picker had no inline rename capability — users had to leave the chat, navigate to the Sessions table, rename there, and return.

## Why This Change Was Made
Inline rename on the picker itself keeps users in their chat context. The implementation reuses the existing `sessions.patch` label contract rather than introducing a new API. Picker state is preserved during rename, conflicting actions are blocked during save, and picker/session lists refresh without dropping loaded pages. The rename-triggered refresh is tied to the picker's request-generation counter so a newer search arriving during the refresh flight is not overwritten. Scope is the Control UI session picker only.

## User Impact
Users can double-click a session name in the chat picker/sidebar to rename it inline, without leaving their conversation. Session rename is available directly where sessions are selected.

## Evidence

target.title: fix(discord): add native reply opt-out
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

- Problem: Discord sends platform-native replies whenever a resolved `replyTo` value is present, but there was no Discord-scoped way for callers to explicitly opt out.
- Solution: Add a Discord-only `useReply` option for `message(action="send")`; `useReply: false` suppresses the native Discord reply reference.
- What changed: The Discord message tool schema now advertises `useReply`, and both Discord send action paths skip `replyTo` when it is false.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Shared message-tool threading, outbound reply inheritance, and non-Discord integrations are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Discord native replies can be useful by default, but some Discord workflows need responses to continue in the current channel or thread without attaching a native reply reference. Before this patch, callers could avoid an explicit `replyTo`, but they could not reliably opt out once a reply target had already been resolved by the surrounding message action flow.

This change keeps the default behavior intact while giving Discord callers a narrow escape hatch: set `useReply: false` to send a normal Discord message instead of a native reply.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord `message(action="send")` can now send without a native Discord reply when `useReply: false` is provided.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with the Discord extension test suite, plus one live Discord send through the patched Discord action handler.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `npx --yes pnpm@11.1.0 exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.messaging.send.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts`
  - `npx --yes pnpm@11.1.0 exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Copied live output from a live Discord API fetch after sending message `1506101861780947064` with both `replyTo` and `useReply: false`:

    ```json
    {
      "ok": true,
      "sentMessageId": "1506101861780947064",
      "channelId": "1506080036107714610",
      "contentMatches": true,
      "hasMessageReference": false,
      "hasReferencedMessage": false,
      "messageReference": null,
      "referencedMessage": null
    }
    ```

  - Targeted Discord tests pass: 3 test files, 93 tests.
  - New tests assert that `message_reference` is omitted when `useReply: false` is supplied.
- Observed result after fix: The Discord send helpers preserve default reply behavior, but omit the native Discord reply target when `useReply` is false.
- What was not tested: A separately packaged release build was not tested; verification used the patched local checkout and live Discord API.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing Discord send paths always forwarded resolved `replyTo` into Discord `message_reference` when present.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord send handling treated any resolved `replyTo` value as an instruction to create a Discord native reply.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no Discord-level schema option or test case for explicitly suppressing native replies.
- Contributing context (if known): Reply target resolution can happen before Discord-specific handling, so the Discord adapter needs its own narrow opt-out.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `useReply: false` is exposed in the Discord message tool schema and prevents Discord `message_reference` from being sent.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is isolated to Discord schema/action handling, so focused Discord tests cover the contract without changing shared message routing tests.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord `message(action="send")` now supports `useReply: false` as a Discord-only option. Defaults are unchanged: if `useReply` is omitted, Discord native replies still work as before.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
message(action="send", replyTo=...) -> Discord send -> native reply

After:
message(action="send", replyTo=...) -> Discord send -> native reply
message(action="send", replyTo=..., useReply=false) -> Discord send -> normal message
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS Darwin 25.2.0 arm64
- Runtime/container: Node.js v25.9.0, pnpm 11.1.0 via `npx --yes pnpm@11.1.0`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Inspect Discord message tool discovery and confirm `action="send"` includes `useReply`.
2. Send through the Discord action path with `replyTo` and default options.
3. Send through the Discord action path with `replyTo` and `useReply: false`.
4. Run the targeted Discord tests.

### Expected

- Default Discord sends keep using native replies when `replyTo` is present.
- Discord sends with `useReply: false` omit the native reply reference.
- Non-Discord message behavior is unchanged.

### Actual

- A live Discord send with both `replyTo` and `useReply: false` created message `1506101861780947064`; reading it back from Discord returned no `message_reference` and no `referenced_message`.
- Targeted tests confirm default reply behavior is preserved.
- Targeted tests confirm `message_reference` is omitted when `useReply: false` is supplied.
- The diff is limited to Discord extension files.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Live Discord verification:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "sentMessageId": "1506101861780947064",
  "channelId": "1506080036107714610",
  "contentMatches": true,
  "hasMessageReference": false,
  "hasReferencedMessage": false,
  "messageReference": null,
  "referencedMessage": null
}
```

Targeted verification:

```text
npx --yes pnpm@11.1.0 exec vitest run \
  extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts

Test files: 3 passed
Tests: 93 passed
```

Formatting:

```text
npx --yes pnpm@11.1.0 exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 \
  extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.messaging.send.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `useReply` is exposed only through the Discord message tool schema contribution.
  - Default Discord `replyTo` behavior is preserved.
  - `useReply: false` suppresses Discord `message_reference` in both Discord send paths.
  - A live Discord send with `replyTo` and `useReply: false` produced no native Discord reply metadata when fetched back from Discord.
  - The diff touches only Discord extension files.
- Edge cases checked:
  - `useReply` omitted behaves like the previous default.
  - `useReply: false` with a present `replyTo` does not forward a native reply reference.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - A separately packaged release build; verification used the patched local checkout and live Discord API.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Callers may assume `useReply` applies to all integrations.
  - Mitigation: The schema description explicitly marks it Discord-only, and the implementation is limited to the Discord extension.
- Risk: Default Discord reply behavior could regress.
  - Mitigation: Tests cover default reply behavior and the explicit opt-out path separately.

target.title: fix(heartbeat-runner): add wakeGuard pre-flight hook to skip no-op wakes (OME-332)
target.body:
## Problem (OME-332)

The `startHeartbeatRunner` wake dispatch path calls `runOnce` for every targeted agent/session without checking whether that agent actually has actionable work. On installations with an orchestration layer (e.g. Paperclip/Ronen brainOS), agents were being woken on every heartbeat interval even when:
- their ticket queue was empty, OR
- the wake context's stored assignee (`heartbeat_runs.contextSnapshot.assigneeAgentId`) didn't match the current agent

This caused no-op LLM invocations on every heartbeat cycle.

## Fix

Add an optional `wakeGuard` hook to `startHeartbeatRunner` opts:

```ts
wakeGuard?: (agentId: string, sessionKey: string | undefined) => Promise<{ skip: boolean }>;
```

The guard fires after deferral evaluation and before `runOnce` in the targeted wake path. Returning `{ skip: true }` suppresses the wake with reason `'wake-guard'`. Callers can use this to:
- Call `getActionableTickets(agentId)` and skip if the result is empty
- Validate `contextSnapshot.assigneeAgentId === agentId` and skip on mismatch

## Properties

- **3-line change** at the dispatch site (the hook plumbing adds a few more in the opts type)
- **100% opt-in**: existing callers not providing `wakeGuard` see zero behaviour change
- **No new dependencies**: the hook is a plain async callback
- Tested: TypeScript compiles; existing heartbeat-runner tests continue to pass (no behaviour change for non-guard path)

## Related

- OME-332: Fix cloud-adapter wake-loop hitting Friday's blocked queue (no-op wakes)
- OME-335: Draft execute.js wake-routing PR

> ⚠️ DRAFT — Omer review needed before merging

target.title: fix(memory-core): treat dreaming fence marker lines as inside-fence in promotion guard (#80613)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** `lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence` in `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts` tracked `insideFence` state from the fence marker lines but never flagged ranges that included the marker lines themselves. A relocated promotion range that ended on a `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:start -->` line, began on a `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:end -->` line, or covered only a marker line passed the guard, and the snippet built from those raw lines carried the marker text into `MEMORY.md`.
- **Why it matters.** The guard's contract is to refuse rehydrated ranges that overlap managed dreaming fences so dream artifacts cannot be promoted. Treating the marker lines themselves as outside-the-fence leaves a narrow but real leak: any window the relocator picks that clips a marker produces a snippet that includes the literal `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:start/end -->` text, and (when the window straddles content on either side of the marker) the adjacent line. This is the residual edge case noted by ClawSweeper on #80702 ("the managed-block leak half is related but should be handled by a focused memory-core promotion fix"), narrowed to the guard itself rather than a parallel sanitizer.
- **What changed.** `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts`: marker lines are now checked against the range bounds; if a marker is inside the relocated range, the range is rejected. The pre-existing inside-fence tracking remains so interior lines between a start/end pair are still flagged. The contract elsewhere — the contamination predicate, the relocator, the apply path, the `MEMORY.md` writer — is untouched.
- **What did not change (scope boundary).** No change to `relocateCandidateRange`, `isContaminatedDreamingSnippet`, `applyShortTermPromotions`, `buildDailySnippetChunks`, `stripManagedDailyDreamingLines`, the CJK dedupe path (the second sub-bug of #80613 is being addressed separately by #80620), or any `MEMORY.md` rendering. No new sanitizer is introduced — this is a narrowing of the existing guard.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

target.title: [codex] Guard doctor repairs for newer configs
target.body:
## Summary

- Block `doctor --fix`, `--yes`, and gateway-token generation from applying repairs when the config was last written by a newer OpenClaw binary.
- Downgrade the doctor prompter to read-only after that guard trips, so later health contributions do not perform repair actions.
- Suppress the generic `Run "openclaw doctor --fix"` hint while version skew is present.

## Why

Running an older checkout against a newer installed Gateway/config can produce stale or misleading repair advice. Doctor should still inspect, but repair guidance and writes should defer to the newer binary unless the existing explicit downgrade override is set.

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-config-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-health.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/doctor-config-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-health.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `pnpm check:changed` was attempted but widened to all lanes because this worktree already has unrelated `.codex/` and `extensions/canvas/src/host/a2ui/.bundle.hash` changes. It failed in the WhatsApp extension typecheck on existing `baileys`/WhatsApp send-key errors outside this PR scope.

target.title: Add Claude ignore rules for generated assets
target.body:
## Summary

- add a repo-level `.claudeignore` so Claude Code skips generated output, dependency/build directories, lockfiles, screenshots, and bulky binary media assets during normal code navigation
- keep source, docs, tests, scoped `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md`, and text SVG assets visible
- include a short note that ignored assets remain in git and can still be requested by exact path when needed

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `python3 ../context-os/python/agent_cost_leak_check.py --repo . --json`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw had repo-level `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md` guidance but no repo-level `.claudeignore`, so Claude Code could still include generated output, lockfiles, screenshots, and bulky media assets during normal repo exploration.

Real environment tested: local shallow clone of `openclaw/openclaw` on branch `add-claudeignore-cost-controls`, using a stdlib local scanner from `sravan27/context-os`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check` and `python3 ../context-os/python/agent_cost_leak_check.py --repo . --json`.

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the after-fix scanner run:

```json
{
  "score": 34,
  "files": 17934,
  "source": 15413,
  "ai": 3,
  "findings": [
    "Large tracked blobs increase read risk",
    "Large source tree needs a repo map",
    "Multi-language repo shape"
  ]
}
```

Before this patch, the same scanner reported score `52/100` and included `No agent ignore file found`. After this patch, that finding is gone and `ai_config_hits` increases from `2` to `3`.

Observed result after fix: `.claudeignore` is present at repo root and covers dependency/build output, package locks, generated docs/localization baselines, screenshots/reports, common binary media assets, and local secret/operator state. After review feedback, global `*.svg` is intentionally not ignored because OpenClaw tracks text-like docs/UI SVG assets.

What was not tested: direct Claude Code runtime proof. I attempted a read-only `claude -p` run with file tools only, but local Claude Code auth returned `401 Invalid authentication credentials` before any tool call, so I am not presenting that as behavior evidence. Runtime behavior, package builds, and broad CI suites were not run because this is a repo metadata change only; no application source code or lockfile content changed.

target.title: fix: Preserve Telegram hooks when flushing buffered finals
target.body:
## Summary
- flush buffered Telegram final answers before returning from segmented final delivery
- preserve the normal preview-finalized `message:sent` hook and transcript mirror when the buffered answer flushes
- add a regression test covering skipped reasoning, visible answer delivery, internal hook emission, and transcript mirroring

## Why this PR exists
This is a hook-preserving replacement for the canonical fix path in #53762. PR #53762 has the right user-visible fix direction, but ClawSweeper flagged that its `flushBufferedFinalAnswer()` path discards the `LaneDeliveryResult`, so a buffered final answer can display in Telegram while bypassing the same preview-finalized hook/transcript path used by ordinary final segment delivery.

I cannot update #53762 directly, so this branch rebases the fix onto current `main` and addresses that review finding in the replacement patch.

## Root cause
When Telegram final output is split into reasoning and answer lane segments, the reasoning segment can be handled/skipped without marking reasoning as delivered. The answer segment is then buffered, but the `segments.length > 0` branch returns before the buffered final answer is drained.

A minimal flush fixes visible Telegram delivery, but the flush also needs to route any `preview-finalized` `LaneDeliveryResult` through `emitPreviewFinalizedHook(...)`; otherwise internal `message:sent` and transcript mirroring diverge from normal final delivery.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram automatic direct-message delivery silently dropped the visible final answer when a final payload split into reasoning and answer lanes left the answer buffered behind a skipped reasoning segment.
- **Real environment tested:** R2/OpenClaw production-style Linux droplet, OpenClaw npm global install `2026.5.12`, systemd user gateway, Telegram channel enabled, real allowlisted direct Telegram chat, direct-message visible replies set to automatic.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Applied the equivalent installed-bundle guard to the live OpenClaw Telegram dispatcher, restarted the gateway, injected a controlled Telegram ingress asking for exact final text `R2_AUTO_OK_SIM`, then ran the live health sequence including `curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:18789/health`, `systemctl --user status openclaw-gateway.service --no-pager -l`, `openclaw gateway status`, and the downstream hotfix checker.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live output: gateway `/health` returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`; the downstream checker printed `OpenClaw Telegram auto-delivery hotfix present`; the Telegram sent-message cache recorded the outbound Bot API delivery at `2026-05-18T12:45:28Z`, message id `27371`; no `message.action` send tool was used.
- **Observed result after fix:** The model final was exactly `R2_AUTO_OK_SIM`, and Telegram automatic delivery sent that visible answer through the Bot API instead of leaving it buffered and unsent.
- **What was not tested:** I did not attach a screenshot or recording of the private Telegram chat; chat details are redacted. The live proof used the installed-bundle equivalent of this source patch, while the exact source branch is covered by the dispatcher regression test below.

Additional redacted proof context is posted on #53762: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/53762#issuecomment-4478724993

## Validation
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-telegram-include-v2.json node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts`
  - Result: `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` passed, 68 tests

Note: I also tried `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, but that CLI-filter form reported `No test files found`, so I am not counting it as validation.

## Related
- Fixes #53384
- Supersedes/addresses the unresolved hook-preservation review finding on #53762
- Replaces closed duplicate #83616

target.title: feat(telegram): add guest mode
target.body: ## Summary - Problem: OpenClaw did not support Telegram Guest Mode, so users could not summon a Telegram bot for a one-off answer from supported chats unless the bot was already participating through normal Telegram message handling. - Why it matters: Guest Mode lets an allowed Telegram user ask the bot for a visible, one-shot answer in a external group or normal 1:1 private chats between users, without turning that chat into a normal OpenClaw conversation. - What changed: Telegram Guest Mode can now be enabled explicitly. Allowed guest requests are routed through an isolated direct-style agent turn, preserve sender metadata, inspect supported current/replied Telegram media when Telegram provides file references, preserve configured Telegram account/channel agent routing, and deliver exactly one visible text response. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Normal Telegram group/direct message handling remains on the existing send path. Guest Mode is disabled by default and does not inherit per-group policies or normal group conversation bindings; it uses account-level Telegram allowlist rules while preserving configured account/channel agent routing. Telegram Guest answers are text/article-only: the Bot API answerGuestQuery endpoint does not accept local file uploads, so generated or local file outputs are described rather than attached in Guest Mode. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #79077 - Partially addresses #79077 - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram bots can answer Guest Bot queries from supported Telegram chats, including groups where the bot is not a member and 1:1 private chats with other users, when `channels.telegram.guest.enabled: true` is configured. - Real environment tested: Ubuntu Linux, local OpenClaw source checkout, systemd user gateway, Telegram channel configured, OpenAI Codex with GPT-5.5. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Activate the guest mode in the BotFather telegram bot settings. 2. Set `channels.telegram.guest.enabled: true`. 3. Built OpenClaw from this branch. 4. Built Control UI assets. 5. Restarted the local gateway. 6. Sent a Telegram guest query to the bot from an (external group where the bot was not a member, or a private chat with another user). (`@openclawbot what time is it? What is my telegram id, what is this channel / group id?`) 7. Verified that one visible answer appeared in Telegram. 8. Verified that users not allowed by the account-level allowlist could not use Guest Mode. 9. Tested groups where the bot is already a member to check that normal Telegram group handling did not regress. - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): - Observed result after fix: The guest query created an isolated direct-style Telegram guest session and produced one visible Telegram answer. Unauthorized guest senders were blocked by account-level allowFrom. Normal groups where the bot is already a member continued to work through the existing Telegram message path. - What was not tested: Bot-to-bot mode, long-running multi-account (more than 3) production soak testing, cross-platform release-package install proof, and cached file_id media-output delivery for Guest Mode. Direct local file upload in answerGuestQuery is not in scope because Telegram does not expose an upload parameter for this endpoint. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, OpenClaw did not include guest_message in Telegram allowed updates and had no answerGuestQuery delivery path, so Guest Bot queries were not converted into agent turns. (So this did just not exist previously) ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: N/A for a new feature. Telegram Guest Bots use a new update and response shape that the existing Telegram channel did not support. - Missing detection / guardrail: Existing Telegram tests covered normal message updates, but not guest_message allowed updates, guest session isolation, or answerGuestQuery delivery. - Contributing context (if known): Guest Mode is similar to inline behavior from a product perspective, but Telegram delivers it as a guest message that must be answered through the Bot API guest-answer endpoint rather than the normal send-message path. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts - extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.test.ts - extensions/telegram/src/probe.test.ts - extensions/telegram/src/webhook.test.ts - Scenario the test should lock in: A guest update is accepted, routed to an isolated guest session, keeps sender metadata available to the agent, preserves configured Telegram account/channel agent routing, does not inherit normal group bindings or group policies, and sends only one visible Telegram guest answer. Guest updates are only requested when Guest Mode is enabled, and guest update queueing is keyed by source chat + sender instead of the normal group lane. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests exercise the Telegram plugin boundary where allowed updates, bot capability detection, guest routing, session context, authorization, and delivery behavior meet, without requiring live Telegram credentials. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A. New tests were added. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Telegram Guest Mode can now be enabled with `channels.telegram.guest.enabled`. - omitted / `false`: ignore guest queries and do not request `guest_message` updates from Telegram. - `true`: request and handle Telegram guest queries. Guest Mode uses the Telegram account-level allowlist. It intentionally does not apply per-group allowlists or normal group conversation bindings because the bot is not a member of the source group in the guest interaction. Guest Mode can inspect supported media included in the guest message or replied message when Telegram file references are available, but generated/local media outputs are not uploaded through answerGuestQuery; the agent should explain that limitation in the one visible text answer. ## Diagram (if applicable) For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`. ```text Before: [Telegram guest_message] -> [not in allowed updates / no guest delivery path] -> [no visible answer] After: [Telegram guest_message] -> [account-level allowlist] -> [isolated direct-style guest session] -> [agent turn] -> [answerGuestQuery] -> [one visible Telegram answer] ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: - New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) Yes. - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No. - New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) Yes. - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No. - Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No. - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This adds a Telegram guest-response capability using Telegram Bot API `answerGuestQuery`. Guest access is gated by the existing Telegram account-level `allowFrom` list, and OpenClaw only requests `guest_message` updates when Guest Mode is explicitly enabled. Guest turns use isolated direct-style session keys, preserve configured Telegram account/channel agent routing, skip group conversation bindings, do not receive source chat history, and deliver only one visible response. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Ubuntu Linux. - Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw source checkout, systemd user gateway. - Model/provider: OpenAI Codex, GPT-5.5. - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram. - Relevant config (redacted): Telegram channel configured with `channels.telegram.guest.enabled: true`; bot token and account details redacted. ### Steps 1. Enable guest mode for the bot using the telegram BotFather. 2. Configure `channels.telegram.guest.enabled: true`. 3. Build OpenClaw from this branch. 4. Build Control UI assets. 5. Restart the local gateway. 6. Send a Telegram guest query to the bot from an external group where the bot is not a member (or a private chat with another user). 7. Confirm that Telegram shows one visible bot answer. 8. Send a guest query from a user that is not allowed by account-level allowFrom. 9. Send normal messages in groups where the bot is already a member. ### Expected - Allowed guest queries create an isolated guest session and produce one visible Telegram answer. - Unauthorized guest senders are blocked. - Normal Telegram group handling still works in groups where the bot is already a member. ### Actual - Allowed guest queries produced one visible answer. - Unauthorized guest senders were blocked. - Existing normal Telegram group handling still worked. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [X] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Live Telegram Guest Mode round trip from a group where the bot was not a member. - Normal Telegram private chats with the bot. - Normal Telegram private chats with other users using the Guest Mode work. - Unauthorized guest sender blocked by account-level allowlist. - Normal Telegram groups where the bot is already a member still handled messages normally. - Control UI assets load after pnpm ui:build. - Local retest after the account-bound guest routing fix. - Edge cases checked: - Unauthorized guest sender fallback. - Guest isolation from normal group bindings. (Needs to remove bot in between from the group) - What you did not verify: - Bot-to-bot mode. - Long-running multi-account (>3) production soak. - Cross-platform release-package install proof. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. Local Codex review was run during the beta-branch iteration and again after moving the patch onto current `origin/main`. Actionable findings from the earlier rounds were addressed before opening the PR. The final main-based Codex review reported no discrete actionable correctness issues. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes. - Config/env changes? (Yes/No) Yes. - Migration needed? (Yes/No) No. ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Telegram Guest Mode supports only one visible answer for this interaction shape. - Mitigation: Guest delivery sends only the first final answer through answerGuestQuery. - Risk: Unauthorized users could trigger guest turns (new ingress style). - Mitigation: Guest authorization uses the Telegram account-level allowFrom list, matching the Telegram DM access boundary. ~Horo
